title: Ensure 'Allow Azure services on the trusted services list to  access this storage account' is Enabled for Storage Account  Access

input_row.title: Ensure 'Allow Azure services on the trusted services list to  access this storage account' is Enabled for Storage Account  Access
input_row.description: NOTE: This recommendation assumes that the Public network access parameter is set to Enabled from selected virtual networks and IP addresses. Please ensure the prerequisite recommendation has been implemented before proceeding: • Ensure Default Network Access Rule for Storage Accounts is Set to Deny If the Allow Azure services on the trusted services list to access this storage account exception is enabled, the following services are granted access to the storage account: • Azure Backup, • Azure Data Box, • Azure DevTest Labs, • Azure Event Grid, • Azure Event Hubs, • Azure File Sync, • Azure HDInsight, • Azure Import/Export, • Azure Monitor, • Azure Networking Services, and • Azure Site Recovery (when registered in the subscription).
input_row.rationale: NOTE: If none of the services listed in the Description are in use in your environment, you may wish to make an exception to this recommendation and disable 'Allow Azure services on the trusted services list to access this storage account.' Turning on firewall rules for a storage account will block access to incoming requests for data, including from other Azure services. Some Azure services that interact with storage accounts operate from networks that can't be granted access through network rules. To help this type of service work as intended, allow the set of trusted Azure services to bypass the network rules. These services will then use strong authentication to access the storage account.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Storage Accounts. 2. For each storage account, under Security + networking, click Networking. 3. Click on the Firewalls and virtual networks heading. 4. Under Exceptions, ensure that Allow Azure services on the trusted services list to access this storage account is checked. Audit from Azure CLI Ensure bypass contains AzureServices az storage account list --query '[*].networkRuleSet' Audit from PowerShell Connect-AzAccount Set-AzContext -Subscription Get-AzStorageAccountNetworkRuleset -ResourceGroupName -Name |Select-Object Bypass If the response from the above command is None, the storage account configuration is out of compliance with this check. If the response is AzureServices, the storage account configuration is in compliance with this check. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: c9d007d0-c057-4772-b18c-01e546713bcd - Name: 'Storage accounts should allow access from trusted Microsoft services'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Storage Accounts. 2. For each storage account, under Security + networking, click Networking. 3. Click on the Firewalls and virtual networks heading. 4. Under Exceptions, check the box next to Allow Azure services on the trusted services list to access this storage account. 5. Click Save. Remediate from Azure CLI Use the below command to update bypass to Azure services. az storage account update --name --resource-group --bypass AzureServices
input_row.impact: This creates authentication credentials for services that need access to storage resources so that services will no longer need to communicate via network request. There may be a temporary loss of communication as you set each Storage Account. It is recommended to not do this on mission-critical resources during business hours.
input_row.default_value: By default, Storage Accounts will accept connections from clients on any network.

step1_initial_assessment:
Based on the analysis of the provided AWS compliance control aligned with the CIS Microsoft Azure Foundations Benchmark, the optimal audit approach is determined as follows:

### AUDIT APPROACH:
**Automated**

### APPROACH DECISION:
- The control can be fully verified via AWS APIs, particularly regarding network access control features for AWS storage services (S3).
- Using AWS APIs (boto3 and AWS CLI commands), we can query the properties related to S3 bucket configurations.
- The mention of how to audit and remediation aligns with AWS practices. For instance, verifying whether public access settings are configured correctly aligns directly with the accessibility controls of AWS services.

### PROGRAM NAME:
aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block_enabled

### AUTOMATION DETAILS:
This automated program would check the following:
- Make API calls using boto3 or AWS CLI commands to check the public access configuration for S3 buckets.
  
**AWS API Calls/Boto3 SDK Methods:**
1. Use `boto3.client('s3')` method to create an S3 client.
2. Use `s3.get_bucket_policy_status(Bucket=bucket_name)` to retrieve the bucket policy status.
3. Use `s3.get_public_access_block(Bucket=bucket_name)` to verify the public access block configuration.

**AWS CLI Commands:**
- To check if public access settings are enabled:
  ```bash
  aws s3api get-public-access-block --bucket <bucket_name>
  ```

**Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
- Enumerate all S3 buckets in the account:
  - Use `s3.list_buckets()` in boto3 or `aws s3api list-buckets` in CLI to get a list of buckets.

**Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
1. Ensure that public access is blocked for all buckets, specifically by checking for the configuration shaped as follows:
   - `BlockPublicAcls: true`
   - `IgnorePublicAcls: true`
   - `BlockPublicPolicy: true`
   - `RestrictPublicBuckets: true`
   
**Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
- Pass: If `BlockPublicAcls`, `IgnorePublicAcls`, `BlockPublicPolicy`, and `RestrictPublicBuckets` are all set to `true`.
- Fail: If any of these settings are `false` or not configured.

This structured approach leverages AWS's capabilities to ensure effective compliance management regarding S3 bucket access control, facilitated through automation and API interactions.
